name: Ram Kumar
title: Senior Frontend Engineer
tagline: Senior frontend engineer building booking systems, dashboards, and animated product UIs in React and Next.js.
summary: Senior frontend engineer with extensive experience designing and scaling modern web applications in healthcare, fitness, and education. I led a frontend team delivering a calendar-based booking system, EMR, and CRM for the SendScript healthcare platform, and built an interactive 3D-animated onboarding UI for the Culnity fitness platform. I'm looking to apply deep React/Next.js expertise and leadership skills to drive high-impact frontend solutions and accelerate product growth.

highlights: Lead a frontend team, coordinating sprint planning and delivery with Agile ceremonies and Git — 95% on-time sprint completion rate
Established code-quality standards and PR workflows with Prettier and ESLint; designed a scalable React architecture using code splitting, cutting bundle size 20%
Mentored developers on performance optimization with React Query caching and animation best practices, reducing page-render time 30%
Drove architectural decisions for modular, high-performance frontend systems with code splitting and lazy loading, raising the Lighthouse score from 78 to 92
Collaborated with product, backend, and design teams to ship modular platforms in React.js and Next.js on a two-week release cadence
Migrated Angular 12 applications to Angular 13, refactoring with TypeScript to reduce technical debt
Built dynamic, schema-validated forms with Angular Forms, including complex conditional logic
Built responsive UI components with Tailwind CSS integrated into a PrimeNg-based design system
Reduced API response time from 20s to 3s — an 85% improvement
Shipped features on the Neo Exam and Neo Hire platforms using Node.js, Express, and Angular
Built 20+ web applications with ASP.NET C# and jQuery using component architecture and modern CSS
Managed SQL Server databases, writing optimized queries and indexing strategies that cut query response time

projects.name: SendScript
projects.description: A calendar-based booking system with a Google Calendar-like UI, built with React.js, code splitting, and animation. I added real-time consultation scheduling with React Query so clinicians see live availability, and integrated OnlyOffice for document automation — cutting contract-generation and processing time. Reusable components and a React Context-based state architecture kept the codebase easy to extend as we added features.
projects.detail:
Sendscript is a modern SaaS-based healthcare platform built to simplify and centralize the entire healthcare journey for both providers and patients. It brings together digital prescription creation, diagnostic scan/test bookings, video consultations, healthcare e-commerce, and bookkeeping in a single interface. What sets it apart is a modular architecture — each feature can run independently, be monetized separately, or be integrated into other platforms via APIs and embeddable SDKs — so it works as a standalone product or as a connected layer inside a larger healthcare ecosystem.

Mission: build a digital healthcare ecosystem that removes the need for multiple disconnected tools, with plug-and-play modularity for maximum flexibility and seamless integration with other healthcare platforms.

Vision: evolve Sendscript into a full EMR system for managing medical workflows, plus a CRM for business operations — finance, patient engagement, service analytics — so everything from clinical to administrative operations runs in one intelligent, cloud-based environment.

Roadmap: work standalone or as part of the full platform; grow into a next-generation, AI-driven EMR/CRM; integrate into third-party systems via APIs, SDKs, or white-labeling; support monetization through individual SaaS modules or a single unified platform.

At full maturity, the goal is to lead the healthcare EMR/CRM space for B2B — clinics, hospitals, health networks — and to be the B2C app patients rely on for their whole healthcare journey: prescriptions, tests, video consults, and medication, all in one place.

My role: I led the frontend build of the booking system's Google Calendar-like UI in React, added real-time consultation scheduling with React Query, integrated OnlyOffice for document automation, and built the reusable component library and React Context-based state architecture that let the team extend the product quickly.

projects.name: Culnity
projects.description: An interactive, 3D-animated onboarding experience built with Theatre.js that made first-time use more engaging and increased time-on-page. I also built the gym discovery and booking flow with React Query and modular components, so users can filter gyms and complete a booking in a few clicks.
projects.detail: Culnity is a fitness platform where I focused on making the first-time experience feel premium rather than transactional. I built an interactive, 3D-animated onboarding flow with Theatre.js — sequenced animations that guide new users through setup and make the app feel alive from the first screen — which increased time-on-page. I also built the gym discovery and booking flow: modular, filterable components backed by React Query so users can browse gyms and complete a booking in a few clicks. Animation-driven UX patterns across the product helped drive repeat visits and higher interaction rates.

projects.name: NeoHire
projects.description: Contributed as one of the frontend developers on NeoHire, Iamneo's AI-powered recruitment automation platform — building UI for skill assessments, AI proctoring, and video interviews with Angular, Node.js, and Express, used by 65+ corporate clients including Fortune 500 companies.
projects.detail:
NeoHire is Iamneo's AI-powered recruitment automation platform, covering the full hiring lifecycle from application to onboarding: custom multi-stage pipelines with eligibility filtering, skill assessments with auto-scoring, AI-powered proctoring (face detection, system locks, real-time monitoring), live and asynchronous video interviews with in-browser coding environments, plagiarism and code-quality analysis, and virtual onboarding with offer and document management. It's used by hiring managers, technical teams, and HR departments at 65+ corporate clients, including Fortune 500 companies, and has assessed over 10 million test-takers.

My role: I was one of the frontend developers building NeoHire's UI, implementing features and flows in Angular, Node.js, and Express.

projects.name: Neo Exam
projects.description: Migrated Neo Exam, Iamneo's AI-proctored exam platform, from Angular 12 to Angular 13, and rebuilt its UI flows — moving from plain CSS to Tailwind CSS with Angular animations and a PrimeNg-based design system — to raise the UI/UX bar for a platform used by 120+ universities.
projects.detail:
Neo Exam is Iamneo's cloud-based, AI-proctored digital examination platform, built to replace paper-based testing with secure, scalable online assessments — AI monitoring (face recognition, object detection, eyeball tracking), multi-layer question security and browser lockdown, support for 50+ programming languages plus MCQ, subjective, and handwritten answers, bulk question uploads, randomized test modules, and real-time analytics. It runs at scale — 200,000+ concurrent users, 5M+ exam hours a year — and is used by 120+ universities.

My role: I migrated Neo Exam's existing Angular 12 codebase to Angular 13. The product originally used plain CSS, so I rebuilt the new UI flows on Tailwind CSS with Angular animations and a PrimeNg-based design system, which lifted the visual polish and interaction quality across the platform.

aiAndLearning: Implementing generative AI in frontend applications
Exploring AI-powered UI and workflow automation
Hands-on with Claude, Ollama, Hugging Face, OpenClaw
achievements: Rookie Neo Award for UI excellence
Delivered features with zero deadline slippage
vision: Growing as a frontend architect / engineering leader
Mentoring developers and improving team productivity
Focused on scalable systems and clean architecture
Goal: build AI-powered products and contribute to open source
